mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market cooling that reduces consumer spending growth expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with declining real savings indicate tightening financial conditions that suppress corporate investment and consumer credit demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS increases and FEDFUNDS rises simultaneously, with a regression coefficient insignificant at p less than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction that reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat following negative INDPRO MoM changes, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases with a t-stat below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS MoM changes falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical relationship with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings crossing above 0.4% force markets to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside falling unemployment indicate strong household formation and consumer confidence, boosting durable goods demand and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns when HOUST increases and UNRATE decreases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant at p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative following GDP prints above 2.0%, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% during mid-cycle phases over a 3-year evaluation window.
